sourceMovie.title: High School Musical
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: High School Musical works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Camp Rock
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If High School Musical clicked for you, Camp Rock (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after High School Musical?
faq.answer: Try Camp Rock, A Cinderella Story: Once Upon a Song, Drumline—each shares DNA with High School Musical while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is High School Musical?
faq.answer: High School Musical (2006) is best described as Comedy, Drama, Family, Music, Romance, TV Movie. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is High School Musical part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "High School Musical Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
